key_insight: An ML-enhanced multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm can discover previously unknown crystal structures by exploring the energy landscape beyond database-limited training data.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable prediction of novel compounds and competing phases in complex materials systems, accelerating design of quantum and magnetic materials.
method: Couple an ANN-based interatomic potential with a multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm.
Use a metadynamics-inspired penalty to avoid revisiting known minima and expand structure search.
Iteratively map both ground states and metastable competitors in composition space.
evidence_signal: The method correctly predicted the Pbam ground state of La4Co4Pb and exactly matched the independently solved orthorhombic structure of La5CoPb2 from x-ray diffraction.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the ANN potential and the search strategy, so difficult chemistries may still require careful tuning and validation.
paper_title: Complex crystal structure prediction using ML-enhanced multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm
